clauses: §Content addressing (steps 1-3 — the hash pins the graph, not its serialization)
note: LWS composition (jeswr/lws-spec docs/alignment/a2a-rdf.md §3): under the JLWS RDF Content Transformation Profile's rdf-1 contract (rdf-transform.html §Transformation semantics — derived representations are graph-isomorphic) a PD stored as Turtle and served as JSON-LD pins identically, because the protocol hash is computed over the parsed graph. This fixture is asserted at generation time to canonicalize to the SAME RDFC-1.0 bytes as pd-hash-grant-access/canonical.nq; the Turtle half of the pair is pd-pin-match (same pinned hash).
title: verify-pd-pin: the SAME PD graph as an rdf-1-faithful JSON-LD rendering matches the Turtle-derived pin (representation-stable pinning)
